title: christella peekelii
intro: Christella peekelii, also known as the Peekel's fern, is a species of fern native to New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Christella peekelii has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune system function. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
